differential_diagnoses: Acute viral myocarditis (Chikungunya or other viral etiology)
Spontaneous bacterial peritonitis with septic shock
Septic shock due to occult infection (e.g., leptospirosis)
next_steps: Send peripheral blood smear for malaria parasites (thick and thin films)
Send serum for Dengue NS1 antigen and IgM/IgG antibodies
Perform diagnostic paracentesis to evaluate for spontaneous bacterial peritonitis
Obtain echocardiogram to characterize biventricular dysfunction and rule out tamponade
Send blood cultures, urine cultures, and testing for leptospirosis
Maintain aggressive hemodynamic support and organ protection in the ICU
Consult infectious disease and hepatology services
